loungeAccess.conditions: Marketed as UNLIMITED complimentary domestic and international lounge access (international via a bundled Priority Pass with 4 additional guest visits/year) for primary and add-on cardholders — the numeric fields above are placeholders for 'unlimited', not literal visit counts. Access requires ₹50,000 spend in the preceding quarter (waived for the first 3 months after card issuance).

history.oldValue: 25,000 bonus EDGE Reward Points for ₹1 lakh+ spend in a calendar month (on top of base earn)
history.newValue: Monthly milestone reward points benefit discontinued

source.name: Paisabazaar
source.document: Axis Magnus Devaluation: Is it still the best travel credit card?

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It carries a joining and annual fee of ₹12,500 + GST, waived from the second year if you spend ₹25 lakh or more in the preceding year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: It can be, for very high spenders and frequent travellers who value unlimited lounge access and can redeem EDGE Reward Points against airline/hotel partners, but the 2025 removal of the monthly milestone bonus has meaningfully reduced its earning potential for big spenders.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: High-income individuals (roughly ₹24 lakh+ annual income) who travel often, want unlimited domestic and international lounge access, and can transfer reward points to airline/hotel loyalty programs.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: No, the standard Mastercard World card is not linkable to UPI.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes — unlimited complimentary domestic and international lounge access (international via a bundled Priority Pass with 4 guest visits/year), conditional on ₹50,000 spend in the preceding quarter (waived for the first 3 months on a new card).
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: Axis Bank's stated eligibility is a minimum annual income of around ₹24 lakh (salaried, or via ITR for self-employed applicants).
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Anyone who can't consistently spend ₹50,000+ a quarter (to keep lounge access active) or ₹25 lakh a year (for the fee waiver), and anyone who primarily wanted the now-discontinued monthly milestone bonus points.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: HDFC Infinia for a comparable ultra-premium travel card, or Axis Magnus for Burgundy if you already bank with Axis Burgundy and can get the fee waived through your relationship.
